examples.title: BOOTSTRAP PROCESS SIMULATION
examples.description: Demonstrates the simplified bootstrap process of an operating system
examples.code:
BEGIN BOOTSTRAP
    // Step 1: Power-On Self Test
    RUN POST
    IF POST FAILS THEN
        DISPLAY ERROR MESSAGE
        HALT
    END IF
    
    // Step 2: Initialize Basic Hardware
    INITIALIZE CPU_REGISTERS
    INITIALIZE INTERRUPT_CONTROLLER
    INITIALIZE MEMORY_CONTROLLER
    
    // Step 3: Load Boot Loader
    READ_BOOT_SECTOR(BOOT_DISK)
    LOAD_INTO_MEMORY(BOOT_LOADER, ADDRESS 0x7C00)
    TRANSFER_CONTROL(BOOT_LOADER)
    
    // Step 4: Load Kernel
    READ KERNEL_FROM_DISK
    PLACE_IN_MEMORY(KERNEL_ADDRESS)
    
    // Step 5: Initialize Kernel Data Structures
    INITIALIZE_PROCESS_TABLE
    INITIALIZE_MEMORY_MANAGER
    INITIALIZE_DEVICE_DRIVERS
    
    // Step 6: Start System Processes
    CREATE_INIT_PROCESS
    CREATE_SWAPPER_PROCESS
    START_SYSTEM_DAEMONS
    
    // Step 7: Display Login
    CLEAR_SCREEN
    DISPLAY "SYSTEM READY"
    SHOW_LOGIN_PROMPT
END BOOTSTRAP
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the bootstrap sequence from power-on through kernel loading to system initialization
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) for fixed number of variables

examples.title: SYSTEM CALL HANDLING MECHANISM
examples.description: Demonstrates how system calls transition from user mode to kernel mode
examples.code:
BEGIN SYSTEM_CALL_HANDLER
    PARAMETERS: syscall_number, arg1, arg2, arg3
    
    // User Space Execution
    FUNCTION user_program()
        result = syscall(WRITE, fd, buffer, count)
        // System call triggers interrupt
    END FUNCTION
    
    // Kernel Space Execution
    INTERRUPT_SERVICE_ROUTINE(trap_interrupt)
        // Save current process state
        SAVE_CPU_REGISTERS_TO_PCB
        SET_CPU_MODE(KERNEL_MODE)
        
        // Lookup system call in table
        syscall_entry = SYSCALL_TABLE[syscall_number]
        
        // Execute appropriate handler
        SWITCH syscall_number OF
            CASE READ: CALL handle_read(fd, buffer, count)
            CASE WRITE: CALL handle_write(fd, buffer, count)
            CASE FORK: CALL handle_fork()
            CASE EXIT: CALL handle_exit(status)
            DEFAULT: RETURN ERROR
        END SWITCH
        
        // Restore process state
        RESTORE_CPU_REGISTERS
        SET_CPU_MODE(USER_MODE)
        RETURN result
    END INTERRUPT
END SYSTEM_CALL_HANDLER
examples.explanation: This shows how a system call causes a mode switch from user to kernel mode, protecting the system from unauthorized access
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for lookup + O(k) for handler

examples.title: SIMPLE SHELL COMMAND PROCESSOR
examples.description: Demonstrates basic command interpreter functionality in operating systems
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates how a command interpreter (shell) parses user commands and makes appropriate system calls to execute them
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of files for ls

examples.title: PROCESS CREATION USING FORK SYSTEM CALL
examples.description: Demonstrates how operating system creates new processes using fork() system call
examples.explanation: This C program demonstrates the fork() system call which creates a new process by duplicating the existing process. The OS creates a Process Control Block for each process and manages them through the kernel
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is memory copied during fork
